head.title: Somnath Dwarka Tour From Bangalore, Flights Included
head.meta_description: Bangalore’s one daily non-stop lands at Rajkot mid-afternoon, so we run Somnath first for the 7:45 show. Flights included. Ground from Rs.15,000 pp.
head.h1: Somnath Dwarka Tour Package From Bangalore, With Flights Included

copy.quick_answer: A Somnath Dwarka tour package from Bangalore is planned around a single flight. Bangalore’s only non-stop into the circuit is one IndiGo service a day to Rajkot Hirasar, departing about 13:15 and landing about 15:30, from around Rs. 6,660. A mid-afternoon landing would strand most circuits, but Rajkot is closest to Somnath and the Somnath sound and light show runs 7:45 to 8:45 pm, so we drive you straight to Somnath and you make the show the same night. The circuit runs Somnath first because of that flight. The ground portion is fixed at Rs. 5,000 per person per night, so four days is Rs. 15,000 per person at 3 star, and with the return flight the all-in is typically around Rs. 28,000 per person, quoted for your dates. If the single daily slot does not suit you, the cheaper Ahmedabad flight is the fallback, at the cost of a long drive.

sections.h2: One flight a day, and it lands in the afternoon
body: The thing that decides a Bangalore trip is not distance, it is the timetable. Bangalore’s only non-stop into this circuit is a single IndiGo flight a day to Rajkot Hirasar, seven a week, about two and a quarter hours, departing around 13:15 and landing around 15:30.

That mid-afternoon landing is the catch most package pages ignore. By the time you clear Hirasar, about 30 km outside Rajkot, it is late afternoon, and a long drive to Dwarka would end well after dark.
So the plan is not to fight the arrival time, it is to use it. And Rajkot’s geography makes that easy.

sections.h2: Somnath first, and the 7:45 show the same night
body: From Rajkot, Somnath is the nearer temple at 197 km, about three and a half hours. Land at 15:30, drive straight down, and you reach Somnath in the evening.

That is the point. The Jay Somnath sound and light show runs 7:45 to 8:45 pm, and the evening aarti is at 7:00 pm, so a Bangalore flyer who lands mid-afternoon still catches the show on the very first night. The late arrival that looks like a problem is the reason to run the whole circuit Somnath first.

From there the trip flows up the coast to Dwarka over the next two days, and the final morning drives back to Rajkot for the afternoon flight home. We time the last darshan around that return, because it too is once a day.
We build the exact first evening around your actual flight, and if the schedule slips we tell you rather than promise a show you might miss.

sections.h2: The cheaper flight, and its catch
body: Bangalore also flies non-stop to Ahmedabad, from around Rs. 5,599, about ten times a day. It is cheaper than the Rajkot flight and far more flexible on timing.

The catch is the drive. Ahmedabad is 440 km from Dwarka, 8 to 9 hours, so a cheaper flight buys you a much longer first day on the road. For most people the single daily Rajkot flight, landing in the middle of the circuit, is worth the small premium.

If your dates cannot match the one Rajkot slot, or you would rather save on the fare, we route you through Ahmedabad and plan the longer drive properly rather than pretend it is short. We lay both options out before you choose.

sections.h2: What all inclusive means here, and what it costs
body: This is one of the package classes with the flight in the price. We book the return flight, the hotels, the breakfast and the vehicle with driver for the full circuit.

The ground portion is fixed. Four days and three nights is Rs. 15,000 per person at the 3 star tier, Rs. 30,000 at 4 star and Rs. 37,500 at 5 star, GST included, with hotel, breakfast, vehicle, tolls, parking, fuel and the driver’s food and stay all inside it.

The flight is the part that moves. Bangalore to Rajkot non-stop has started around Rs. 6,660 each way and changes with the date and the booking window, so a four day all-in at 3 star typically lands around Rs. 28,000 per person. The Ahmedabad routing brings the flight down toward Rs. 5,599 each way, though the drive grows. We quote the exact combined figure for your dates.
On Janmashtami and Diwali both halves rise, the ground rate up to threefold and airfares with it, so those dates are booked about three months ahead.

days.title: Day 1: Bangalore to Rajkot, then straight to Somnath for the show

stops.text: The single daily IndiGo non-stop leaves Bangalore for Rajkot Hirasar, about 2 hours 15 minutes.
Land at Hirasar. We meet you and drive straight for Somnath, the nearer temple at 197 km, rather than the longer haul to Dwarka.
Reach Somnath in the evening, in time for the 7:00 pm aarti and the 7:45 pm sound and light show, the first night’s payoff for a mid-afternoon arrival.
Check in at Somnath at your tier. Night one done, and the circuit is already underway.

days.title: Days 2 and 3: The middle of the trip, shared by every start city
days.intro: Once you are on the circuit your start city stops mattering, so rather than reprint the same two days on every origin page we hand them to the plan that owns them. From Bangalore the order runs Somnath first, so these two days flow up the coast toward Dwarka. Two full days of Dwarka, Bet Dwarka across Sudarshan Setu, Nageshwar and the coastal run through Porbandar, set out hour by hour on the four day plan. The full hour by hour version of both days sits on the four day plan, and the drive-from-Rajkot detail is on the Rajkot page. For a Bangalore arrival we simply run it Somnath first. See /somnath-dwarka-tour-package/4-days-3-nights/

days.title: Day 4: Dwarka morning, then the timed drive to the afternoon flight

stops.text: Early Dwarkadhish darshan and the last of the shore temples.
Drive back toward Rajkot Hirasar, 225 to 235 km, timed to the single daily return flight.
Rajkot Hirasar for the non-stop back to Bangalore. Because the return runs once a day, the morning is planned tight and unhurried rather than open ended.
Home in Bangalore the same day.

price_sheet.intro: Two parts, one quote. The ground portion is fixed per person per night. The return flight is added at the live fare, and the combined figure is quoted for your dates.

table.hotels: The Fern Sattva Resort at Dwarka, best property in town at Somnath, named in your quote

price_sheet.all_in_note: All in note. The all-in figures assume a return Bangalore to Rajkot non-stop from around Rs. 6,660 each way. The Ahmedabad routing brings the flight down toward Rs. 5,599 each way at the cost of a longer drive. Airfare moves with the date, so we quote the exact combined number for your dates.
price_sheet.gst_note: Gst note. Every ground price here is final and includes GST. Nothing is added at checkout beyond the flight, which is shown to you at its actual fare.
price_sheet.group_and_child: Group and child. The ground rate is per person on twin sharing. In a group of about six the 3 star ground rate falls to around Rs. 4,000 per person per night on triple sharing, and to around Rs. 3,500 in a group of about twelve. A solo traveller pays the base plus Rs. 3,500 for the same services. Children under five travel free on the ground portion, and from five they are counted as an adult. Airfare is per seat at whatever the airline charges for the age.
price_sheet.vehicle_note: Vehicle note. Group size picks the vehicle. Sedan for 2 to 3, Ertiga for 4 to 5, Innova Crysta for 5 to 6, 9 seater Tempo Traveller for 7 to 9, 12 seater Maharaja or Urbania for 10 to 12, 26 seater Tempo Traveller above that.
price_sheet.included_note: Included note. The package covers the return Bangalore flight, the hotel at your tier, breakfast, the vehicle with driver for the full circuit, and all road tolls, parking, fuel and the driver’s food and stay. You land to a car and a plan.
price_sheet.five_star_honesty: Five star honesty. No five star Taj or ITC property operates in Dwarka or in Somnath. In Dwarka the top tier puts you in The Fern Sattva Resort. At Somnath it buys the best room in town, named in your quote before you pay. Taj and ITC enter your plan only on the legs that reach them, which this circuit does not.
price_sheet.blackout_note: Blackout note. Two dates break this rate card. On Janmashtami and Diwali, ground demand can push prices up to three times these numbers and airfares climb with them, and we take a limited number of bookings made about three months ahead. If your travel lands on either, ask early and we will quote the real figure rather than a floor you cannot get.
price_sheet.exclusions: Lunch and dinner, personal pooja, seva and abhishek at either temple, the sound and light show ticket, camera and cloakroom charges, tips, in-flight meals, seat selection and excess baggage charged by the airline, and anything you buy yourself. The Gir safari permit is extra if you add Gir.
price_sheet.cancellation: Cancellation. We confirm a booking on 50 percent advance, and you pay the remaining 50 percent after you arrive, during the journey. The flight follows the airline’s own fare rules, which we tell you before you pay. Cancellation, refunds and the payment schedule for the ground portion are set out in full on our cancellation and refund policy, and the booking terms sit alongside it. Read both before you pay.
price_sheet.trust_warning: Trust warning. The Shree Somnath Trust accepts pooja and donation bookings only through its own website and warns that it never asks for payment through private numbers, WhatsApp or UPI. Book sevas yourself at somnath.org.

why_choose: We plan around the single daily flight instead of ignoring it, so your first evening is the Somnath show, not a dark drive.
We know Rajkot is closest to Somnath and use it, turning a mid-afternoon landing into a same-night darshan.
One price, one booking. The return flight, the hotels, the breakfast and the car are all ours to arrange.
We give you the cheaper Ahmedabad option honestly, drive and all, rather than hiding the tradeoff.
A local Gujarat team meets your flight and drives the circuit. One number, +91 7302265809, on call and WhatsApp.
We reconfirm the flight and every timing before departure, because the schedule and the show both matter here and both can move.
copy.not_for_you_if_heading: This trip is not for you if
not_for_you_if: You want a wide choice of flight times into the middle of the circuit. There is one Rajkot non-stop a day from Bangalore, and the trip is built around it.
You want to land nearest Dwarka. Bangalore has no non-stop to Jamnagar, so the nearest airport to Dwarka needs a connection.
You want one fixed all-in price months out regardless of the airfare. The ground half is fixed, the flight half moves, and an honest all-in is quoted per date.
You want to fly in and see both temples in a day. Even with a Somnath-first evening, both temples need the full four days.
You want a five star Taj in Dwarka. It does not exist, and anyone selling you one should be asked for the property name.

faq.q: Is there a direct flight from Bangalore to Dwarka or Jamnagar?
faq.a: There is no airport at Dwarka, and Bangalore has no non-stop to Jamnagar, the airport nearest it. Bangalore’s non-stop into the circuit is a single daily IndiGo flight to Rajkot Hirasar, about 225 km from Dwarka and 197 km from Somnath, which is why we route this package through Rajkot.

faq.q: Which airport should I fly into from Bangalore for Dwarka and Somnath?
faq.a: Rajkot Hirasar for the trip itself, because it is the most central airport and closest to Somnath, on the single daily non-stop. Ahmedabad is cheaper and more frequent but 8 to 9 hours from Dwarka. We recommend Rajkot unless the one daily slot does not fit your dates.

faq.q: How do I reach Dwarka from Bangalore by flight?
faq.a: Fly the daily Bangalore to Rajkot Hirasar non-stop, landing about 15:30, and we drive you straight to Somnath for the evening show, then up the coast to Dwarka over the next days. The alternative is the cheaper Ahmedabad flight with a much longer drive.

faq.q: How much is a Dwarka Somnath package from Bangalore with flights?
faq.a: The ground portion is fixed at Rs. 15,000 per person for four days at 3 star, GST included. With a return Bangalore to Rajkot flight from around Rs. 6,660 each way, the all-in is typically around Rs. 28,000 per person, and we quote the exact figure for your dates.

faq.q: How many days do you need for Dwarka and Somnath from Bangalore?
faq.a: Four days and three nights works, and five is more relaxed, because the flight runs once a day each way and fixes the arrival and departure windows. The Somnath-first evening means you start darshan on day one despite the afternoon landing.

final_cta.summary: One flight a day into the middle of the circuit, landing mid-afternoon, so we run Somnath first and you catch the 7:45 show the first night. Ground fixed at Rs. 15,000 per person at 3 star, all-in typically around Rs. 28,000, quoted for your dates. Cheaper Ahmedabad routing on request.
